compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Implemented Option B in worktree bugfix/sidecar-prune-lock. Added event_append.delete_events(tracker, relpaths, msg): acquires write_lock, checks rebase guard, git rm + PATHSPEC-scoped git commit (git commit -- <paths>, never whole-index), routes git through _with_index_lock_retry, restores deletions on commit failure. Routed all four sidecar prunes (plan_review, code_review, completion, overlap/digest) through it; each keeps its best-effort try/except. New test tests/unit/test_store_delete_events.py (4 cases incl. the anti-sweep property: a concurrent writer's staged blob is NOT swept into the prune commit). Local: lint + typecheck green; targeted store tests + new tests green (27 passed). Full make test running for CI pre-flight.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Pushed to Gerrit as change 715: https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/715 (commit 224ab3f, based on origin/main 7a0fac8f5). Local pre-flight all green: make lint + make typecheck + make test (6024 passed, 33 skipped). Awaiting LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1 before closing + submitting.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary
The four LLM sidecar retention prunes bypass rebar's canonical locked event-write
path. Each `prune()` runs raw `git rm` + a whole-index `git commit` (no pathspec)
via `subprocess.run`, WITHOUT acquiring `write_lock`, WITHOUT rebase-guard, and
WITHOUT the shared index-lock retry/self-heal. This races normal ticket writes in
the shared tracker worktree.

Affected sites (near-identical copy-paste):
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py` `prune`
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` `prune`
- `src/rebar/llm/completion_sidecar.py` `prune`
- `src/rebar/llm/overlap/digest_sidecar.py` `prune`

Contrast the canonical writer `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py:stage_and_commit`,
which holds `write_lock`, checks rebase state, does a path-limited add, retries
index-lock failures, and cleans staged residue. event_append even warns (line
~263) that a whole-index commit "could sweep unrelated staged residue in under
THIS write's message" — the prunes reintroduce exactly that, outside the lock.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Run an LLM gate (plan-review / completion-verify / code-review) whose sidecar
   emit triggers `prune()` on a ticket with > RETAIN_PER_TICKET sidecar events.
2. Concurrently run a normal ticket write (claim/transition/comment) on the same
   tracker so its `git add` (holding write_lock) overlaps the prune's lock-free
   `git commit`.

Expected: retention prune is serialized with every other store write; it commits
only its own deletions and never observes/writes another writer's staged state.

Actual: the prune's whole-index `git commit` can commit a concurrent writer's
staged event under a `prune:` message, causing the original writer's commit to
report a false failure (its `_unstage`+`_silent_unlink` then removes an
already-committed event from the worktree, diverging worktree from HEAD). Under
index.lock contention the prune instead fails outright (best-effort swallowed),
making retention unreliable in the multi-agent workload.

## Fix (Option B — narrow lock-respecting delete helper)
Add ONE `delete_events(tracker, relpaths, commit_msg)` primitive in
`event_append` that acquires `write_lock`, checks the rebase guard, `git rm`s the
given paths, commits PATHSPEC-SCOPED (`git commit -- <paths>`, not whole-index),
and routes git through the shared index-lock retry. Route all four prunes through
it. Keep the helper NARROW (delete-events-under-lock, not a general locked-git
facade) and preserve each caller's best-effort try/except so a failed prune never
fails the gate.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A single `delete_events`-style helper exists in `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py`
      that acquires `write_lock`, checks the rebase guard, and routes git through the
      shared index-lock retry.
- [ ] The helper's `git commit` is pathspec-scoped to the deleted paths (cannot
      sweep unrelated staged residue), and it cleans up on failure.
- [ ] All four sidecar `prune()`s (`plan_review/sidecar.py`, `code_review/sidecar.py`,
      `completion_sidecar.py`, `overlap/digest_sidecar.py`) call the helper instead of
      raw `subprocess.run(["git", ... "rm"/"commit"])`.
- [ ] Each caller retains its best-effort exception-swallowing posture (a failed
      prune never fails the gate).
- [ ] A unit/interface test covers the helper: it deletes only the named events,
      commits under the lock, and does not sweep an unrelated staged file in the index.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass; the change lands via Gerrit
      with `LLM-Review +1` and `Verified +1`.
compiled_state.title: Sidecar retention prunes bypass the canonical locked write path (unlocked git rm+commit races store writes)
